failure_surface: AttributeError: 'Cell' object has no attribute 'formula' when attempting to set cell.formula = None
root_cause: The agent attempted to explicitly set cell.formula = None in openpyxl, but direct formula attribute assignment requires specific handling. In openpyxl, formulas should be stored as value strings starting with '=' rather than using a separate formula attribute.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Store formulas by setting cell.value to the formula string (prefixed with '=') instead of using cell.formula. When writing non-formula values, only assign cell.value without touching a formula attribute.
evidence: Error occurred at line: cell.formula = None after setting cell.value
openpyxl Cell objects don't expose formula as a writable attribute in the manner attempted
The working alternative code used cell.value = col_info['raw_value'] where raw_value includes formula strings already formatted correctly
Successful execution log shows the corrected version completed without AttributeError

memory_items.title: Formula Storage Pattern in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: Formulas in openpyxl should be assigned via cell.value as strings starting with '=' rather than using cell.formula attribute
memory_items.generalization: When storing formulas, use cell.value = '=FORMULA_STRING'. Do not attempt to set cell.formula as a separate property.

memory_items.title: API Attribute Discovery Before Use
memory_items.lesson: Verify library-specific attributes exist before assignment, especially for properties that vary between versions
memory_items.generalization: Before writing to object attributes in spreadsheet libraries, confirm they support both read and write operations via testing or documentation.

memory_items.title: Value-Only Assignment for Non-Formula Cells
memory_items.lesson: When clearing formulas during overwrite, simply assign the new value without explicit formula nullification
memory_items.generalization: To replace a formula with a value, only set cell.value. The underlying structure handles format transitions automatically.

edits.content:
## Formula Handling
- **Storage Method**: Store formulas as value strings starting with '=' (e.g., `cell.value = '=SUM(A1:A10)'`) rather than using cell.formula attribute.
- **Clearing Formulas**: To remove a formula and store a value, simply assign to cell.value; do not attempt to set cell.formula = None.
- **Read/Write Consistency**: When reading formulas, check if cell.value starts with '='. When writing, preserve this format for formula cells.
